rulings.Case Title: TEDDA HUGHES VS. FRANK & ME LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Amended Complaint And Request For Attorney'S Fees And Costs,
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 2, 2009, line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANT FRANK & ME LLC, and, LAUREN BLACKWELDER'S Special Motion To Strike Amended Complaint And Request For Attorney's Fees And Costs. Motion to strike is denied. Defendant fails to establish the communication is protected activity. Insufficient showing that when communication made a lawsuit was under serious consideration. See A.F. Brown vs. Rhino Electric Supply (2006) 137 Cal.App. 4th 1118. Objection to Blackwelder declaration is overruled. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE SPAGNUOLO et al VS. WENJIAN WANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-09-486336 WITH CASE CGC-08-480216
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 2, 2009, line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT CHRISTINE SPAGNUOLO'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-09-486336 WITH CASE CGC-08-480216. Granted. No opposition filed. Matter is consolidated for all purposes with Case No. 486336. Lead case No. is 480216. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHANIE ENGELBRECHT VS. EDWARD ROBINSON et al

rulings.Case Title: BIOMARIN PHARMACEUTICAL INC., A DELAWARE VS. LA JOLLA PHARMACEUTICAL COMPANY, A DELAWARE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 2, 2009, line 12, Plaintiff's Motion for Preliminary Injunction. Motion is denied. Plaintiff unlikely to prevail on the merits. Stock purchase agreement doesn't provide for the relief requested. No set time limit provided for other than 90-day deadline in section 6.1A(2). =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. WILLIAM DAWSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 2, 2009, line 8, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion To Tax Costs. Motion to tax costs is granted in part, and denied in part. Defendant is the prevailing party and is entitled to recover costs. Insufficient showing that the writ filing fee and service of process costs are unneccessary or unreasonable. $788.25 costs are taxed due to clerical error. =(302/CWW/Arn)

rulings.Case Title: PEARL INVESTMENT COMPANY LLC VS. BRUCE O BRAMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, June 2, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT BRUCE BRAMAN'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer is overruled. Defendant received written notice of the breach in January 2009 and was provided an opportunity to cure at that time. Plaintiff is in compliance with San Francisco Administrative Code 37.9(a)(2). The Court strikes the words "Doe 1" from paragraph 6(a) pursuant to CCP section 436. =(302/CWW/PB)
